title: acer negundo subsp. mexicanum
intro: Acer negundo subsp. mexicanum, also known as the Mexican boxelder, is a subspecies of the boxelder tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Acer negundo subsp. mexicanum include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to create remedies for colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
